entries.input_requirement: Copy: contestant intros, voting/elimination text, episode details

generator.prompt:
Write the season-launch copy pack for 'The Lock House' reality competition.
Return a JSON object with keys:
"contestants": EXACTLY 6, each an object with "name" (invented first name + surname, diverse cast),
"age_desc" (e.g. 24, bartender from a fictional town), "tagline" (<=7 words, big personality),
"intro" (30-40 words, third person, dramatic);
"episode": object with number (1), "title", "synopsis" (40 words), "voting_text" (how viewers vote,
includes a short code format like VOTE NAME to 5-55-55), "elimination_text" (suspenseful 25 words);
"ctas": list of 3 short calls-to-action for posts/banners.
Voice: Our tone is intensely dramatic and suspenseful, aiming to create a sense of high-stakes intrigue. It should feel bold and energetic, reflecting the competitive nature and emotional intensity of the show.

entries.input_requirement: Show logo ('The Lock House') + brand colours

generator.prompt:
Cinematic reality-TV show logo: bold logotype reading EXACTLY "THE LOCK HOUSE" with
an integrated padlock/keyhole motif. Colors: #00566B, #000000, #FFD700 on a deep dark background. Glossy
broadcast key-art finish, dramatic metallic lighting on the letterforms, perfectly legible, no tagline,
no other text.

Dramatic reality-TV cast portrait: Anya Sharma, 29, high-stakes poker player from Oakhaven, exuding the vibe "Bluffing her way to the top.". Waist-up studio shot on a moody dark-teal seamless background with a single rim light and bold key light, confident direct-to-camera gaze, styled streetwear-smart wardrobe. Consistent cast-photoshoot look across the season. Invented person, no real-celebrity likeness. ABSOLUTELY NO text, letters, numbers, watermarks, logos or symbols anywhere in the image.

Dramatic reality-TV cast portrait: Marcus "Mac" Thorne, 35, ex-military strategist from Port Blossom, exuding the vibe "Discipline and strategy define his game.". Waist-up studio shot on a moody dark-teal seamless background with a single rim light and bold key light, confident direct-to-camera gaze, styled streetwear-smart wardrobe. Consistent cast-photoshoot look across the season. Invented person, no real-celebrity likeness. ABSOLUTELY NO text, letters, numbers, watermarks, logos or symbols anywhere in the image.

Dramatic reality-TV cast portrait: Isabella "Izzy" Rossi, 22, aspiring fashion designer from Silverwood, exuding the vibe "Underestimate her at your peril.". Waist-up studio shot on a moody dark-teal seamless background with a single rim light and bold key light, confident direct-to-camera gaze, styled streetwear-smart wardrobe. Consistent cast-photoshoot look across the season. Invented person, no real-celebrity likeness. ABSOLUTELY NO text, letters, numbers, watermarks, logos or symbols anywhere in the image.

issues: The rim light on the hair, while present, is slightly too uniform and intense, creating a somewhat artificial 'halo' effect rather than a natural light fall-off, which slightly detracts from absolute photorealism.

Flat sponsor logo #1 of 2 for a reality TV show's lower-third and banner placements.
Sponsor #1 is "VOLTRA" (a fictional energy drink — angular electric wordmark, lime + black).
Sponsor #2 is "StreamNest" (a fictional streaming app — rounded friendly wordmark + play-nest icon,
coral + navy). Render ONLY the sponsor for this image number, exact spelling, flat vector on white
background, no taglines, no other text. No real-world brands, trademarks, celebrity or athlete likenesses, or recognizable team logos.

A previous attempt FAILED review. Avoid these exact issues:
- Not a flat vector, appears to have shading and 3D effects.
- Logo is not clean due to multiple gradients and shadows.
- The size and legibility at small scale is questionable due to intricate details.
- Background is white, which is correct.
- Text is spelled correctly as VOLTRA.

issues: The logo uses green and black colors, not flat single color vector.
The logo has black outlines which may reduce legibility at very small sizes.
The style is somewhat complex with lightning bolt shapes integrated into letters, which may affect small-size legibility.
